page: Vultr vcg-mi325x-252c-2872g-1536vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server configured with an x86_64 CPU architecture providing 252 vCPUs under a shared allocation model. The system features 2872.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 11.4 GB of RAM per core.

This server is equipped with six AMD MI325X GPUs from the CDNA3 family, delivering a total of 1536 GB of VRAM. For local storage, the configuration includes 14336 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Additionally, the instance is provisioned with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the massive GPU memory footprint and high-density system memory, this hardware profile is designed for extreme resource density. The qualitative cost efficiency is centered on consolidating massive parallel workloads onto a single bare-metal host rather than distributing them across multiple smaller instances. This configuration is suited for large-scale GPU-accelerated workloads, such as large language model training, high-performance computing, and massive data science jobs.
description: Vultr vcg-mi325x-252c-2872g-1536vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server designed for high-density parallel computing. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 252 vCPUs with shared allocation and 2872.0 GB of system memory, providing 11.4 GB of RAM per core. The server is equipped with six AMD MI325X CDNA3 GPUs, offering a total of 1536 GB of VRAM, alongside 14336 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This hardware profile is optimized for workloads requiring massive GPU memory capacity and high-speed local storage. The consolidation of six high-capacity GPUs and substantial system memory on a single bare-metal host provides high resource density for demanding computational tasks. It is suited for large-scale artificial intelligence workloads, deep learning model training, and complex data science simulations.
